meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: How to Solve Coding Blocks for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to leverage freelance pitching and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio and ship projects.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop spinning your wheels. Shift your focus to freelance pitching—offering small, AI-assisted fixes to real-world clients. This forces you to solve concrete problems, accelerates your technical mastery, and builds a professional portfolio. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn complex bugs into shipable features.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your code refuses to compile. You are staring at a wall of red error messages, feeling the familiar, sinking weight of 'I can't fix this.' Every developer has been here, but the difference between an amateur and an engineer is how they navigate this exact moment of paralysis.

Instead of spiraling, you are going to pivot. We are transforming your frustration into a professional asset. By adopting a freelance pitching mindset, you stop viewing bugs as roadblocks and start viewing them as service opportunities. Let's turn your technical hurdles into your first paid project and your strongest portfolio entry.

steps.title: Audit Your Technical Debt
steps.description: Identify the exact error preventing your app from launching. Framing this as a specific constraint is the first step in pitching it as a solvable project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the primary architectural bottleneck preventing deployment. Provide a high-level summary of the bug and the technical requirements for a fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Explain why my build is failing and suggest a refactor strategy.'

steps.title: Draft a Value-Driven Pitch
steps.description: Don't sell 'coding services'; sell 'solutions to broken workflows.' Pitch your ability to fix the bug as a way for a business owner to save time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a cold outreach email for a potential client that highlights my ability to fix [Bug Name] in their tech stack, emphasizing the value of a stable, production-ready app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a professional pitch template for a freelance developer offering bug-fix services.'

steps.title: Simulate the Fix Environment
steps.description: Before promising a fix, ensure you can replicate the solution. Use your AI tools to test the fix in a sandboxed environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction script for the bug identified in step 1 and outline the steps for a permanent patch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm test' or 'python main.py' to confirm current failure state.

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Apply the fix in small, verifiable increments. This ensures you maintain control over the codebase and can easily revert if something breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a step-by-step refactoring plan to resolve the bug, focusing on modular changes that minimize side effects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'Apply the recommended refactor to the specific module causing the crash.'

steps.title: Document and Showcase
steps.description: Turn the solved bug into a case study. Documenting your process is the best way to attract future clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md summary of the bug, my technical approach, and the final solution for my portfolio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a case study summary for my portfolio based on the changes made in this session.'

pro_tips: Never pitch a fix without first offering a free 15-minute diagnostic audit; it lowers the barrier to entry for the client.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your fixes; clients pay more for code that comes with a safety net.
When you get stuck, use Antigravity to explain the code back to you in plain English; explaining the logic often reveals the bug.
Keep a 'Bug Journal' of every error you solve; this becomes your secret weapon during technical interviews.
Automate your pitch follow-ups; the money in freelancing is almost always in the third or fourth follow-up email.
content.next_steps: After completing your first fix, deploy your project to a public repository, share the link on your LinkedIn profile, and reach out to three local businesses offering a free technical audit.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to turn any error into a professional success story. Start pitching, keep shipping, and don't look back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
